reasoning: Hamas has explicitly and recently rejected immediate disarmament, stating it is a complex process that will take considerable time and depends on numerous conditions (e.g., arrangements for leader safety, security mechanisms). The strict resolution criteria explicitly exclude plans contingent on future conditions. While Phase 2 negotiations of the U.S.-backed peace plan are underway, historically the disarmament phase of a peace process for an entrenched militant group involves prolonged and difficult negotiations. Reaching a formal, unconditional policy agreement on disarmament within the remaining 7 weeks of the year is highly unlikely.
batch_queries: Will Hamas agree to disarm by December 31?
